entries: Animated objects are crafted with potent magic to follow the commands of their creators. When not commanded, they follow the last order they received to the best of their ability, and can act independently to fulfill simple instructions. Some animated objects (including many of those created in the Feywild) might converse fluently or adopt a persona, but most are simple automatons.
Constructed Nature. An animated object doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

The magic that animates an object is dispelled when the construct drops to 0 hit points. An animated object reduced to 0 hit points becomes inanimate and is too damaged to be of much use or value to anyone.

entries.name: Senses
entries: Blindsight 60 ft. (blind beyond this radius), passive Perception 6

entries.name: Traits
entries: Antimagic Susceptibility. The armor is incapacitated while in the area of an antimagic field. If targeted by dispel magic, the armor must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against the caster's spell save DC or fall unconscious for 1 minute.
False Appearance. While the armor remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal suit of armor.

A flying sword dances through the air, fighting with the confidence of a warrior that can't be injured. Swords are the most common weapons animated with magic. Axes, clubs, daggers, maces, spears, and even self-loading crossbows are also known to exist in animated object form.

entries.text: Would-be thieves and careless heroes arrive at the doorsteps of an enemy's abode, eyes and ears alert for traps, only to end their quest prematurely as the rugs beneath their feet animate and smother them to death.

A rug of smothering can be made in many different forms, from a finely woven carpet fit for a queen to a coarse mat in a peasant's hovel. Creatures with the ability to sense magic detect the rug's false magical aura.

In some cases, a rug of smothering is disguised as a carpet of flying or another beneficial magic item. However, a character who stands or sits on the rug, or who attempts to utter a word of command, is quickly trapped as the rug of smothering rolls itself tightly around its victim.

items: Antimagic Susceptibility. The rug is incapacitated while in the area of an antimagic field. If targeted by dispel magic, the rug must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against the caster's spell save DC or fall unconscious for 1 minute.
entries.text: Damage Transfer. While it is grappling a creature, the rug takes only half the damage dealt to it, and the creature grappled by the rug takes the other half.
False Appearance. While the rug remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a normal rug.

action.name: Detect
entries: {@check wis percep}

action.name: Tail Attack
entries: {@atk mw} +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d8+6} piercing damage.

trait.name: Damage Resistances
entries: Resistance to necrotic damage. While in dim light or darkness, the dragon has resistance to damage that isn't force, psychic, or radiant.

action.name: Breath Weapon
entries: {@atk mw} +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d6+2} slashing damage.

entities.name: Varian T: Drag Ons as Innate Spellcasters

trait.name: Lair
entries: Black dragons dwell in swamps on the frayed edges of civilization. A black dragon's lair is a dismal cave, grotto, or ruin that is at least partially flooded, providing pools where the dragon rests, and where its victims can ferment. The lair is littered with the acid-pitted bones of previous victims and the fly-ridden carcasses of fresh kills, watched over by crumbling statues. Centipedes, scorpions, and snakes infest the lair, which is filled with the stench of death and decay.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: Can breathe air and water.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: {@atk mw} +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d6+2} slashing damage.

trait.name: Capricious Hunters
entries: A green dragon hunts by patrolling its forest territory from the air and the ground. It eats any creature it can see, and will consume shrubs and small trees when hungry enough, but its favorite prey is elves.

actionTags: Legendary Resistance (3/day). If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Breath Weapon
entries: The dragon exhales a cone of fire 60 feet long. Each creature in the area must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking 54 (12d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The red dragon makes three attacks with its claws or one attack with its bite.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} +16 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 2d6+8 slashing damage}.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} +16 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 15d6+12 piercing damage}.

action.name: Breath Weapon
entries: The red dragon uses its fiery breath in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes {@damage 15d6 fire damage} and is {@condition burning} for 1 round. On a success, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't burning.

action.name: Hoarder's Instinct
entries: The red dragon knows the value and provenance of every item in its hoard, along with each item's exact location. It might notice the absence of a single coin, igniting its rage as it tracks down and slays the thief without mercy.

trait.name: Noble Curiosity
entries: Metallic dragons covet treasure but are driven to investigate and collect unclaimed relics. Their hoard reflects their persona and history, and they protect others from dangerous magic. Powerful magic items or evil artifacts may be secreted away in their hoard.

action.name: Natural Armor
entries: AC 17 (natural armor)

action.name: Damage Immunities
entries: Fire

action.name: Senses
entries: Blindsight 30 ft., Darkvision 120 ft., passive Perception 20

action.name: Languages
entries: Common, Draconic

action.name: Saving Throws
entries: Dex +5, Con +8, Wis +5, Cha +7

action.name: Skills
entries: Perception +10, Persuasion +7, Stealth +5

trait.name: Sunlight Holes
entries: Ceiling holes allow sunlight to enter.

action.name: Lair Features
entries: The lair is typically a ruin, canyon, or cave network.

action.name: Detect
entries: The dragon makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.

trait.name: Good Hosts
entries: A copper dragon appreciates wit, a good joke, humorous story, or riddle. A copper dragon becomes annoyed with any creature that doesn't laugh at its jokes or accept its tricks with good humor.

trait.name: Fond of Bards
entries: A copper dragon might carve out part of its lair as a temporary abode for a bard willing to regale it with stories, riddles, and music. To a copper dragon, such companionship is a treasure to be coveted.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: Can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Devourer of Wealth
entries: Gold dragons can eat just about anything, but their preferred diet consists of pearls and gems. Thankfully, a gold dragon doesn't need to gorge itself on such wealth to feel satisfied. Gifts of treasure that it can consume are well received by a gold dragon, as long as they aren't bribes.

entries.name: Drider Lore
entries: When a drow shows great promise, Lolth summons it to the Demonweb Pits for a test of faith and strength. Those that pass the test rise higher in the Spider Queen's favor. Those that fail are transformed into driders- a horrid hybrid of a drow and a giant spider that serves as a living reminder of Lolth's power. Only drow can be turned into driders, and the power to create these creatures resides with Lolth alone.

Scarred for Life. Drow transformed into driders return to the Material Plane as twisted and debased creatures. Driven by madness, they disappear into the Underdark to become hermits and hunters, either wandering alone or leading packs of giant spiders.

On rare occasion, a drider returns to the fringes of drow society despite its curse, most often to fulfill some longstanding vow or vendetta from its former life. Drow fear and shun the driders, holding them in lower esteem than slaves. However, they tolerate the presence of these creatures as living representatives of Lolth's will, and a reminder of the fate that awaits all who fail the Spider Queen.

items: Driders that were once drow spellcasters might retain their ability to cast spells. Such driders typically have a higher spellcasting ability (Wis 15 or 16) than other driders. Further, the drider gains the Spellcasting trait. A drider that was a drow divine spellcaster, therefore, could have a Wisdom of 16 (+3) and a Spellcasting trait as follows.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Spellcasting
entries: The drider is a 7th-level spellcaster. Its spellcasting ability is Wisdom (spell save DC 14, +6 to hit with spell attacks). The drider has the following spells prepared from the cleric spell list:
Cantrips (at will): poison spray, thaumaturgy
1st level (4 slots): bane, detect magic, sanctuary
2nd level (3 slots): hold person, silence
3rd level (3 slots): clairvoyance, dispel magic
4th level (2 slots): divination, freedom of movement

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: Can breathe air and water.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: {@atk mw} +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d6+2} slashing damage.

action.name: Whirlwind
entries: {@atk mw} +4 to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. Hit: {@damage 2d8} bludgeoning damage. The target is pushed 10 ft. away. If the target is a creature, it can't take actions on its next turn.

trait.name: Pinpoint Location
entries: The elemental can pinpoint the precise location of any foe that stands on solid ground in its vicinity.

action.name: Fire Spike
entries: {@atk} +5 to hit, one target. Hit: {@damage 2d6+3} fire damage. The target must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, or take 2d6 fire damage and be{@condition burned}.

action.name: Air Form
entries: The elemental can enter a hostile creature's space and stop there. It can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.

action.name: Water Jet
entries: The elemental can make one melee attack with its body. On a hit, it deals 2d6 bludgeoning damage. The target must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be pushed 10 feet away.

action.name: Water Walk
entries: The elemental can move through water without needing to breathe, and it can move through water as though it were solid ground.

Drow elite warriors defend their houses and their superiors against all enemies, although they specialize in fighting dwarves, gnomes, and elves (including other drow). They frequently raid surface settlements under cover of night, returning to the Underdark with prisoners and spoils in tow before dawn. Elite warriors can be male or female.

Privileged drow males who lack the strength and fighting prowess to train as warriors have no recourse but to pursue the study of magic. For them, it is a matter of survival. Female drow with a natural affinity for the arcane arts may also become drow mages, although they are much less common.

entries.name: Drow Priestess of Lolth
entries: Female drow with blood ties to a noble house are molded and trained from birth to become priestesses of Lolth. The Spider Queen doesn't allow male drow to hold such positions. Such priestesses execute the will of the Spider Queen, and as a result, they wield tremendous power and influence in drow society. The matron mothers who rule the drow houses are the most powerful of Lolth's priestesses, but they must constantly balance their devotion to the Spider Queen with their devotion to...

entries.name: Armor Class
entries: 15 (chain shirt)

entries.name: Speed
entries: 30 ft.

trait.name: Superior Invisibility
entries: As a bonus action, the dragon can magically turn invisible until its concentration ends (as if concentrating on a spell). Any equipment the dragon wears or carries is invisible with it.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: Can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Gargantuan
entries: A gargantuan giant has advantage on saves against poison and fire damage.

action.name: Greatclub
entries: {@atk mw} +5 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 2d8+2} bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: Can breathe air and water.

action.name: Mist Form
entries: {@atk sw} +5 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d8+3} bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The fire giant makes two attacks with its greataxes or one attack with its greataxes and one with its greatclub.

trait.name: Primitive
entries: Hill giants dwell in hills and mountain valleys... poorly stitched together with hair and leather thongs.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The giant has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

action.name: Innate Spellcasting
entries:
The giant's innate spellcasting ability is Charisma. It can innately cast the following spells, requiring no material components:

At will: detect magic, fog cloud, light
3/day each: feather fall, fly, misty step, telekinesis
1/day each: control weather, gaseous form

action.name: Spellcasting
entries: {@atk mw} +8 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d8+8} lightning damage.

action.name: AC
entries: 18 (plate)

action.name: Speed
entries: 30 ft.

trait.name: Draconic Resistance
entries: Resistance to destructive energy

trait.name: Breath Weapon
entries: Ability to exhale a breath weapon

trait.name: Draconic Nature
entries: Cannot have natural offspring; must use magic to propagate
Long lifespan (twice that of nondraconic line)

trait.name: Personality Traits
entries: Half-gold dragons: shy and secretive
Half-copper dragons: impish and playful
Half-green dragons: deceitful
Half-white dragons: dim-witted brutes
Greedy, arrogant, and paranoid tendencies

trait.name: Special Senses
entries: Enhanced perception abilities

action.name: Breath Weapon
entries: The half-dragon has the breath weapon of its dragon half. The half-dragon's size determines how this action functions.
Size  Breath Weapon
Large or smaller  As a wyrmling
Huge  As a young dragon
Gargantuan  As an adult dragon

action.name: Resistances
entries: The half-dragon gains resistance to a type of damage based on its color.
Color  Damage Resistance
Black or copper  Acid
Blue or bronze  Lightning
Brass, gold, or red  Fire
Green  Poison
Silver or white  Cold

action.name: Senses
entries: The half-dragon gains blindsight with a radius of 10 feet and darkvision with a radius of 60 feet.

trait.name: Psionic Ability
entries: A gray ooze that lives a long time can evolve to become more intelligent and develop limited psionic ability. Such occurrences are more common in gray oozes that live near psionic creatures such as mind flayers, suggesting that the ooze can sense and mimic psionic ability.

action.name: Psychic Crush
entries: {@atk melee} The ooze targets one creature that it can sense within 60 feet of it. The target must make a DC 10 Intelligence saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. (Recharge 5-6)

action.name: Slime
entries: {@atk melee} The ooze makes one melee attack with its body. The target must succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw, or take 10 (3d6) acid damage.

trait.name: Slithers
entries: When it moves, it slithers like a liquid snake, fising to strike.

action.name: Digestive Enzymes
entries: {@atk} +3 to hit, range 30 ft., one creature. Hit: {@damage 1d6} acid damage. The target must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, or it is diseased. The disease deals 1d6 acid damage at the start of each of the target's turns until the disease is cured. The target can use an action to cure the disease.

trait.name: Familiar
entries: The pseudodragon can serve another creature as a familiar, forming a magic, telepathic bond with that willing companion. While the two are bonded, the companion can sense what the pseudodragon senses as long as they are within 1 mile of each other. While the pseudodragon is within 10 feet of its companion, the companion shares the pseudodragon's Magic Resistance trait. At any time and for any reason, the pseudodragon can end its service as a familiar, ending the telepathic bond.

entries.name: Revenant
entries.content: A revenant forms from the soul of a mortal who met a cruel and undeserving fate. It claws its way back into the world to seek revenge against the one who wronged it. The revenant reclaims its mortal body and superficially resembles a zombie. However, instead of lifeless eyes, a revenant's eyes burn with resolve and flare in the presence of its adversary. If the revenant's original body was destroyed or is otherwise unavailable, the spirit of the revenant enters another humanoid corpse. Regardless of the body the revenant uses as a vessel, its adversary always recognizes the revenant for what it truly is.

entries.name: Hunger for Revenge
entries.content: A revenant has only one year to exact revenge. When its adversary dies, or if the revenant fails to kill its adversary before its time runs out, it crumbles to dust and its soul fades into the afterlife. If its foe is too powerful for the revenant to destroy on its own, it seeks worthy allies to help it fulfill its quest.

entries.name: Divine Justice
entries.content: No magic can hide a creature pursued by a revenant, which always knows the direction and distance between it and the target of its vengeance. In cases where the revenant seeks revenge against more than one adversary, it pursues them one at a time, starting with the creature that dealt it the killing blow. If the revenant's body is destroyed, its soul flies forth to seek out a new corpse in which to resume its hunt.

entries.name: Undead Nature
entries.content: A revenant doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

entries.name: Regeneration
entries.content: The revenant regains 10 hit points at the start of its turn. If the revenant takes fire or radiant damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of the revenant's turn.

entries.name: Rejuvenation
entries.content: After 24 hours, the revenant's soul inhabits another humanoid corpse.

entries.name: Turn Immunity
entries.content: A revenant is immune to the effects of turning, binding, and similar magical effects.

entries.name: Vengeful Tracker
entries.content: A revenant always knows the direction and distance to its adversary, regardless of magical interference.

entries.name: Revenant
entries.content:
**Armor Class** 13 (natural armor)
**Hit Points** 136 (16d8 + 64)
**Speed** 30 ft.

**Ability Scores**
- Strength: 16
- Dexterity: 12
- Constitution: 14
- Intelligence: 8
- Wisdom: 14
- Charisma: 10

**Saving Throws**
- Constitution +5
- Wisdom +4

**Damage Resistances**
- Necrotic
- Piercing
- Bludgeoning

**Damage Immunities**
- Poison
- Fire

**Condition Immunities**
- Poisoned
- Charmed
- Frightened

**Senses**
- Passive Perception 14

**Languages**
- Common
- Ancient

**Challenge**
- 10 (160 XP)

entries.name: Revenant
entries.content: The revenant's curse is a reminder of the ancient pact between life and death, where the soul is bound to seek vengeance until its purpose is fulfilled.

entries.name: Gythka
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) slashing damage.

entries.name: Chameleon Carapace
entries: The thri-kreen can change the color of its carapace to match the color and texture of its surroundings. As a result, it has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide.

entries.name: Standing Leap
entries: The thri-kreen's long jump is up to 30 feet and its high jump is up to 15 feet, with or without a running start.

languageTags: one language known by its creator
one language known by its creator

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} +6 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 14 (3d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Representative Statistics
entries: Use the stat block of one animal to represent another. Example: Panther for jaguar, giant goat for buffalo, hawk for falcon.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The baboon has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the baboon's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The badger has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Echolocation
entries: The bat can't use its blindsight while deafened.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} +0 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: {@damage 1} piercing damage.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The bear has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The dog has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Keen Sight
entries: The hawk has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the boar moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a tusk attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 3 (1d6) slashing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 11 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

action.name: Tusk
entries: {@atk mw} +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d6+2} slashing damage.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The bear has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The cat has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The crocodile can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

trait.name: Two-Headed
entries: The dog has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks and on saving throws against being blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, stunned, or knocked unconscious.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The wolf has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Keen Sight
entries: The eagle has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the elephant moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a gore attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 12 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the elephant can make one stomp attack against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Charge
entries: If the elk moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then rams it with a ram attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The snake doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

action.name: Charge
entries: If the boar moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a tusk attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} slashing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The crab can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The crocodile can hold its breath for 30 minutes.

trait.name: Keen Sight
entries: The eagle has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the elk moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a ram attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 7 (2d6) damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The beetle sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The frog can breathe air and water.

action.name: Standing Leap
entries: The frog's long jump is up to 20 feet and its high jump is up to 10 feet, with or without a running start.

action.name: Charge
entries: If the goat moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a ram attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra {@damage 5 (2d4)} bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

trait.name: Rampage
entries: When the hyena reduces a creature to 0 hit points with a melee attack on its turn, the hyena can take a bonus action to move up to half its speed and make a bite attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d8+2} piercing damage.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The lizard can hold its breath for 15 minutes. (A lizard that has this trait also has a swimming speed of 30 feet.)

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The lizard can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: While out of water, the octopus can hold its breath for 1 hour.

action.name: Underwater Camouflage
entries: The octopus has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made while underwater.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The owl doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Disease
entries: Until the disease is cured, the target can't regain hit points except by magical means, and the target's hit point maximum decreases by 3 (1d6) every 24 hours. If the target's hit point maximum drops to 0 as a result of this disease, the target dies.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 3 (1d4 + 1)} piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw or contract a disease.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d4+2} piercing damage.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The rat has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Charge
entries: If the sea horse moves at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a ram attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 11 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

trait.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: The shark has advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its hit points.

action.name: Spider Climb
entries: The spider can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The toad can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The toad's long jump is up to 20 feet and its high jump is up to 10 feet, with or without a running start.

trait.name: Keen Sight and Smell
entries: The vulture has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight or smell.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The weasel has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The spider can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Sure-Footed
entries: The goat has advantage on Strength and Dexterity saving throws made against effects that would knock it prone.

action.name: Ram
entries: {@atk melee} +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 2d6+1} bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. On a failed save, the target also takes 2 (1d4) extra bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: The shark has advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its hit points.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The hyena has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the hyena's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The jackal has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

action.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The jackal has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the jackal's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Echolocation
entries: The whale can't use its blindsight while deafened.

action.name: Pounce
entries: If the lion moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the lion can make one bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the mammoth moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a gore attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 18 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the mammoth can make one stomp attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Beast of Burden
entries: The mule is considered to be a Large animal for the purpose of determining its carrying capacity.

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: While out of water, the octopus can hold its breath for 30 minutes.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The owl doesn't provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Sight
entries: The owl has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or sight.

trait.name: Blood Frenzy
entries: The quipper has advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its hit points.

trait.name: Water Breathing
entries: The quipper can breathe only underwater.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The rat has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Mimicry
entries: The raven can mimic simple sounds it has heard, such as a person whispering, a baby crying, or an animal chittering. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations with a successful {@dc 10} Wisdom (Insight) check.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The shark has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the shark's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Water Breathing
entries: The sea horse can breathe only underwater.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The spider can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny snake. The swarm can't regain hit points or gain temporary hit points.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The tiger has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

action.name: Pounce
entries: If the tiger moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a claw attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the tiger can make one bite attack against it as a bonus action.

trait.name: Trampling Charge
entries: If the horse moves at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hits it with a hooves attack on the same turn, that target must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. If the target is prone, the horse can make another attack with its hooves against it as a bonus action.

action.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The weasel has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The wolf has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Smell
entries: The worg has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or smell.

trait.name: Nimble Escape
entries: The goblin can take the Disengage or Hide action as a bonus action on each of its turns.

action.name: Scimitar
entries: {@atk mw} +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 1d6+2} slashing damage.

trait.name: Spellcasting
entries: The acolyte is a 1st-level spellcaster. Its spellcasting ability is Wisdom (spell save DC 12, +4 to hit with spell attacks). The acolyte can cast the following cleric spells prepared: Cantrips (at will): light, sacred flame, thaumaturgy; 1st level (3 slots): bless, cure wounds, sanctuary.

entities.name: Archmage

action.name: Armor Class
entries: 12 (15 with mage armor)

keywords: Magic Resistance: has advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects
Magic Resistance: has advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects

trait.name: Reckless
entries: At the start of its turn, the berserker can gain advantage on all melee weapon attack rolls during that turn, but attack rolls against it have advantage until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Dark Devotion
entries: The cultist has advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

trait.name: Dark Devotion
entries: The fanatic has advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

trait.name: Spellcasting
entries: The druid is a 4th-level spellcaster. Its spellcasting ability is Wisdom (spell save DC 12, +4 to hit with spell attacks). It has the following druid spells prepared: Cantrips (at will): druidcraft, produce flame, shillelagh; 1st level (4 slots): entangle, longstrider, speak with animals, thunderwave; 2nd level (3 slots): animal messenger, barkskin

trait.name: Any Alignment
entries: Medium humanoid (any race), any alignment

trait.name: Passive Perception
entries: passive Perception 12

trait.name: Brave
entries: The knight has advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

trait.name: Divine Eminence
entries: As a bonus action, the priest can expend a spell slot to cause its melee weapon attacks to magically deal an extra 10 (3d6) radiant damage to a target on a hit. This benefit lasts until the end of the turn. If the priest expends a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the extra damage increases by 1d6 for each level above 1st.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Sight
entries: The scout has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or sight.

trait.name: Cunning Action
entries: On each of its turns, the spy can use a bonus action to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

action.name: Sneak Attack
entries: The spy deals an extra 7 (2d6) damage when it hits a target with a weapon attack and has advantage on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally of the spy that isn't incapacitated and the spy doesn't have disadvantage on the attack roll.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The thug has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the thug's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally is not incapacitated.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The warrior has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the warrior's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.
